g.text: Sharing the trust anchor is necessary but not sufficient. Authorization is by SAN, not by mere possession
of a cert signed by agentry-ca.
Per-Agent certs are signed by the very same CA and differ only in their SAN,
so the SAN check is the whole control: it rejects the compromised agent before any handler runs. The same
agent cert earns the same 403 on the gateway's /v1/activity and /v1/channels/health.
Why the listeners do not simply require a client cert.
Neither can. The controller's :9443 is shared with
kubelet's cert-less /healthz and /readyz probes, and the gateway's :8443 must let gateway-only-tier callers
complete a handshake and authenticate with a bearer token on adjacent paths. Both therefore run
alone is not sufficient, precisely because both listeners accept cert-less connections by design.
Two distinct rejections at the handler:
no client cert at all gives
